summary: Frontend-only UI readability testing for Global Readability Hardening (P1). All 6 pages tested render correctly without errors. Verified: Admin Dashboard, Admin Orders, Admin Payments, Admin Service Tasks, Partner Dashboard, and Customer Invoices pages.
critical_code_review_comments: Global Readability Hardening CSS in index.css (lines 243-283) correctly applies: table headers slate-700 with font-weight 600, table cells slate-700, status badges font-weight 600, form labels slate-800 with font-weight 500, inputs slate-800
StatusBadge.jsx uses font-semibold class for all badges - verified working
MetricCard.jsx uses text-slate-600 font-medium for labels, text-[#20364D] font-bold for values - good contrast
AdminDashboardV2.jsx KpiCard uses text-slate-600 font-semibold for labels - verified working
VendorDashboardPage.jsx KpiCard uses text-slate-500 font-semibold for labels - verified working
InvoicesPageV2.jsx table headers use uppercase text-xs tracking-wide - verified working
All pages compile without errors (only React hooks exhaustive-deps warnings which are non-blocking)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Global Readability Hardening P1 verified. All admin pages (Dashboard, Orders, Payments, Service Tasks), partner pages (VendorDashboardPage), and customer pages (InvoicesPageV2) render correctly with improved text contrast. CSS changes in index.css apply globally to table headers, badges, labels, and inputs.
